brief.name: Etsy Image Helper
brief.target_user: Etsy sellers who need store graphics and listing visuals to fit cleanly without trial and error.
brief.core_problem: Sellers waste time resizing, cropping, and re-exporting Etsy images because every surface has different aspect ratios and minimum size expectations.
secondary_intent: etsy mini banner vs big banner
brief.tool_output: Recommended Etsy dimensions, crop guidance, quality warning, and a copyable prep summary.
risk_notes: describe dimensions as guidance and preparation support

homepage.hero_title: Etsy image helper for cleaner crops and faster exports
homepage.hero_intro: Match the right Etsy surface, catch weak source files early, and stop re-exporting the same artwork until it finally fits.
homepage.lede: This site combines a live Etsy image helper with focused guides for listing photos, banners, icons, thumbnails, and shop branding surfaces.
homepage.value_intro: The value is speed to a cleaner export workflow, not inflated page count.
value_points: See the best-fit export target for each major Etsy surface.
Catch weak source files before you waste time polishing them.
Move from sizing question to finished export with a checklist and linked guides.
homepage.next_step: Start with the live tool, then use the linked guides to tighten the crop strategy for each surface.
workflow_steps: Pick the Etsy surface you are preparing.
Enter the current width and height.
Copy or print the result before exporting the final artwork.
homepage.who_for_body: Use this when you manage your own Etsy visuals and need one practical reference point for listing photos, banners, icons, and profile assets.
homepage.tool_preview_body: The tool returns recommended dimensions, aspect ratio guidance, source-quality warnings, and crop notes before you export.
homepage.faq_preview_title: Fast answers to common Etsy image questions
homepage.faq_preview_intro: These are the image questions that usually trigger re-export loops in the middle of a shop update.
homepage.comparison_preview_title: Banner and photo choices worth checking
homepage.comparison_preview_intro: Mini vs big banners and square vs vertical images change the way your artwork behaves across Etsy surfaces.
homepage.resource_preview_intro: Use the checklist when you want the same prep rhythm across your shop refresh, banner swap, and product upload sessions.

tool_page.title: Etsy image helper tool
tool_page.intro: Choose a target surface, enter the current width and height, and get instant crop and resize guidance for Etsy-friendly exports.
tool_page.inputs_intro: Keep the input set lean so you can check a file in seconds while you are actively exporting.
tool_page.result_intro: The result combines recommended dimensions with a quality verdict.
tool_page.result_detail: You also get a crop note, resize note, and a copyable summary you can drop into your editing checklist.
trust_paragraphs: Treat the helper as a prep assistant, not as a replacement for visual judgment. The goal is to get your file into the right ratio and quality range before you fine-tune the composition.
If the tool flags a risky source, go back to the largest original available instead of trying to rescue a small export.
tool_page.trust_callout: Static-first rule: everything here runs in the browser, so you can use the tool on a draft machine or during a batch content sprint without any account setup.
next_steps: Copy or print the summary before uploading.

about_page.title: Why Etsy image prep turns into a time sink
about_page.lede: Most sellers are not missing creativity. They are missing a stable preparation system that keeps every export aligned to the right ratio from the start.
about_page.takeaway: One square-first master and a short preset list beat endless last-minute resizing.
about_page.detail: Once you separate listing imagery, banners, icons, and profile photos into clear surface rules, your brand files become much easier to update without visual drift.
about_page.next_action: Use the helper to verify the next file you plan to upload, then keep the checklist beside your editor so every export follows the same logic.

feedback_page.title: What Etsy image workflow should come next?
feedback_page.intro: This release stays static-first, so the feedback route is a notes page that defines the best next signals to collect once the site earns a lightweight intake flow.
prompts: Which Etsy surface still causes the most resize churn?
Would a crop simulator or a template pack help more next?
Which part of the upload workflow still feels fragile after using the helper?
feedback_page.callout: Keep this page honest until a real winner earns a live form integration.

faq_topics.question: What size should Etsy listing photos be?
faq_topics.short_answer: Use a square-first workflow and export at 2000 by 2000 pixels when you can. Etsy will accept smaller images, but large square files hold up better in crop-heavy layouts.
faq_topics.reason: The first listing image gets squeezed into thumbnails, mobile grids, and zoom views. Oversized square exports give you more room to crop without losing clarity.
faq_topics.next_step: Run the tool with your current photo size and confirm whether your source is safely above the recommended square target.

faq_topics.question: What size is an Etsy shop icon?
faq_topics.short_answer: Prepare the shop icon as a square and export at 500 by 500 pixels. Keep the logo or mark centered so it survives circular or tight crops.
faq_topics.reason: Small branding surfaces punish edge-heavy artwork. A centered square icon keeps your mark readable in the app, on desktop, and in small account previews.
faq_topics.next_step: Check the shop icon preset in the tool before exporting any rebrand assets.

faq_topics.question: How wide is an Etsy big banner?
faq_topics.short_answer: A big banner works best at 1600 by 400 pixels, which gives you a clean 4 to 1 layout. Wider source files are fine as long as the important content stays in the middle band.
faq_topics.reason: Horizontal banners break fast when text or products sit too close to the edges. Knowing the target ratio up front saves you from awkward last-minute crops.
faq_topics.next_step: Compare your source image to the big banner preset and trim the safe area before you export.

faq_topics.question: Should I use the mini or big banner on Etsy?
faq_topics.short_answer: Use a mini banner when you want a thin branding strip and a big banner when the header itself needs to carry messaging or product context.
faq_topics.reason: The two banner styles are not interchangeable. Each one rewards a different layout approach, so forcing the same art into both usually looks cramped.
faq_topics.next_step: Review the comparison page before committing to one banner layout for your shop refresh.

faq_topics.question: Why do Etsy thumbnails crop weirdly?
faq_topics.short_answer: Thumbnails usually look wrong when the product sits too close to an edge or the source aspect ratio fights the square-first listing layout.
faq_topics.reason: Etsy compresses wide and tall photos into smaller previews. Centering the subject and leaving breathing room around the edges makes the crop feel intentional instead of accidental.
faq_topics.next_step: Use the tool and the thumbnail guide together to decide whether you need a recrop or a fresh export.

faq_topics.question: Can I fix a small Etsy image with upscaling?
faq_topics.short_answer: Upscaling can help in a pinch, but it is safer to start from the largest original you have. Enlarging a weak source often softens detail and makes text or edges look fuzzy.
faq_topics.reason: Etsy surfaces are visual trust signals. Soft artwork makes the whole listing feel less polished, even if the dimensions technically pass.
faq_topics.next_step: Let the tool flag risky source files before you spend time editing them.

guides.title: Etsy listing photo size guide for square-first product shots
guides.lede: Build listing photos around a square master so one export can handle gallery views, thumbnails, and zoom states without surprise crops.
guides.takeaway: Start with the cleanest square crop you can and keep the product slightly inside the edges.
guides.detail: Square exports make batch editing easier, reduce thumbnail guesswork, and let you reuse the same crop logic across collections.
guides.next_action: Use the listing photo preset in the tool and compare your current width and height before you upload.

guides.title: Etsy banner safe area guide for text, logos, and product cutouts
guides.lede: Banner dimensions matter, but safe-area discipline matters more. The center band should carry the real message while the edges stay quiet.
guides.takeaway: Treat the outer edges as disposable space and keep text inside the middle third of the artwork.
guides.detail: This approach protects both big banners and mini banners from awkward crops and lets you reuse seasonal artwork with fewer edits.
guides.next_action: Check the banner presets in the tool and line up your focal point before exporting.

guides.title: Etsy thumbnail crop tips that keep the product readable on mobile
guides.lede: Mobile grids punish edge-to-edge compositions. The fastest fix is to crop with breathing room and keep the focal object centered.
guides.takeaway: Leave margin around the hero product so the square thumbnail does not amputate it.
guides.detail: Even simple items photograph better when the product sits in a stable crop zone with negative space around it.
guides.next_action: Run your image through the tool, then compare the ratio advice to your current composition.

guides.title: Etsy shop icon design rules for logos that survive small screens
guides.lede: Shop icons are tiny trust markers. Clear shapes, high contrast, and centered artwork outperform detailed illustrations every time.
guides.takeaway: Strip the icon down to one recognizable mark and let negative space do the work.
guides.detail: Small avatars fail when the design relies on fine text or thin decorative elements that disappear on mobile.
guides.next_action: Use the icon preset and confirm your export size before you rebuild the brand mark.

guides.title: Etsy profile photo sizing workflow for founder shots and studio portraits
guides.lede: Profile photos should feel warm, legible, and tightly composed. Start with a square crop and make sure the face or subject stays centered.
guides.takeaway: Keep eyes and facial features away from the edges so compression does not cut them off.
guides.detail: Even a simple square portrait looks stronger when the subject is framed with a small margin instead of filling the whole image.
guides.next_action: Check the profile preset in the tool and match the recommended square size before exporting.

guides.title: Etsy order receipt banner setup for clean post-purchase branding
guides.lede: The order receipt banner is narrow, so it works best with restrained branding and a single message.
guides.takeaway: Use bold text, simple contrast, and one focal element instead of a busy collage.
guides.detail: Narrow receipt banners collapse fast when they try to carry the full shop identity. Think confirmation strip, not homepage hero.
guides.next_action: Use the receipt banner preset and export a lean layout built for the 760 by 100 shape.

comparisons.title: Etsy big banner vs mini banner for handmade shops
comparisons.lede: Both banner types can work, but they solve different jobs. One is a statement piece and the other is a lightweight identifier.
comparisons.option_one_body: Choose the big banner when the header needs to feel cinematic or when you want to show product context, a tagline, or seasonal art.
comparisons.option_two_body: Choose the mini banner when the shop header just needs a fast brand strip that stays out of the way of listings and categories.
comparisons.decision_shortcut: If the banner must communicate more than a logo, pick the big banner. If it only needs to reinforce branding, pick the mini banner.

comparisons.title: Etsy shop icon vs profile photo and when each matters
comparisons.lede: The icon sells the brand mark while the profile photo sells the person behind the shop. Treat them as separate trust layers, not duplicates.
comparisons.option_one_body: Use the icon for logos, monograms, or simple illustrations that need to look consistent across every Etsy surface.
comparisons.option_two_body: Use the profile photo when your shop story matters and you want buyers to connect with the maker behind the products.
comparisons.decision_shortcut: If the surface needs brand recognition, prioritize the icon. If it needs warmth or founder credibility, prioritize the profile photo.

comparisons.title: Etsy square photos vs vertical photos for catalog consistency
comparisons.lede: Square photos are easier to manage at scale, while vertical photos can feel more editorial when the product demands height.
comparisons.option_one_body: Use square exports when you want consistent thumbnails, faster batch editing, and less unpredictable cropping in category views.
comparisons.option_two_body: Use vertical compositions when the product needs height, but protect the center area so the image still survives square previews.
comparisons.decision_shortcut: Default to square for repeatable storefront systems and switch to vertical only when the product story clearly improves.

resource.title: Etsy image prep checklist
resource.lede: Download a plain-English prep checklist before your next listing update or shop rebrand.
resource.resource_contents: A surface-by-surface export checklist covering listing photos, banners, icons, profile photos, and receipt branding.
resource.usage_steps: Review the target surface, export from the largest source available, confirm the ratio in the tool, and keep focal content inside the safe area.
resource.next_action: Pair the checklist with the helper tool so you catch weak source files before you upload.

pages.metaDescription: Upscaling can help in a pinch, but it is safer to start from the largest original you have. Enlarging a weak source often softens detail and makes text or edges look fuzzy.

sections.heading: Direct answer
paragraphs: Upscaling can help in a pinch, but it is safer to start from the largest original you have. Enlarging a weak source often softens detail and makes text or edges look fuzzy.

sections.heading: Why it matters
paragraphs: Etsy surfaces are visual trust signals. Soft artwork makes the whole listing feel less polished, even if the dimensions technically pass.

sections.heading: Next step
paragraphs: Let the tool flag risky source files before you spend time editing them.

pages.metaDescription: Build listing photos around a square master so one export can handle gallery views, thumbnails, and zoom states without surprise crops.

sections.heading: Key takeaway
paragraphs: Start with the cleanest square crop you can and keep the product slightly inside the edges.

sections.heading: Practical detail
paragraphs: Square exports make batch editing easier, reduce thumbnail guesswork, and let you reuse the same crop logic across collections.

sections.heading: Next action

pages.title: Etsy banner safe area guide for text, logos, and product cutouts
pages.description: Banner dimensions matter, but safe-area discipline matters more. The center band should carry the real message while the edges stay quiet.
pages.heroTitle: Etsy banner safe area guide for text, logos, and product cutouts
pages.heroIntro: Banner dimensions matter, but safe-area discipline matters more. The center band should carry the real message while the edges stay quiet.
pages.metaTitle: Etsy banner safe area guide for text, logos, and product cutouts | Etsy Image Helper
pages.metaDescription: Banner dimensions matter, but safe-area discipline matters more. The center band should carry the real message while the edges stay quiet.

sections.heading: Key takeaway
paragraphs: Treat the outer edges as disposable space and keep text inside the middle third of the artwork.

sections.heading: Practical detail
paragraphs: This approach protects both big banners and mini banners from awkward crops and lets you reuse seasonal artwork with fewer edits.

sections.heading: Next action
paragraphs: Check the banner presets in the tool and line up your focal point before exporting.

pages.title: Etsy thumbnail crop tips that keep the product readable on mobile
pages.description: Mobile grids punish edge-to-edge compositions. The fastest fix is to crop with breathing room and keep the focal object centered.
pages.heroTitle: Etsy thumbnail crop tips that keep the product readable on mobile
pages.heroIntro: Mobile grids punish edge-to-edge compositions. The fastest fix is to crop with breathing room and keep the focal object centered.
pages.metaTitle: Etsy thumbnail crop tips that keep the product readable on mobile | Etsy Image Helper
pages.metaDescription: Mobile grids punish edge-to-edge compositions. The fastest fix is to crop with breathing room and keep the focal object centered.

sections.heading: Key takeaway
paragraphs: Leave margin around the hero product so the square thumbnail does not amputate it.

sections.heading: Practical detail
paragraphs: Even simple items photograph better when the product sits in a stable crop zone with negative space around it.

sections.heading: Next action
paragraphs: Run your image through the tool, then compare the ratio advice to your current composition.

pages.title: Etsy shop icon design rules for logos that survive small screens
pages.description: Shop icons are tiny trust markers. Clear shapes, high contrast, and centered artwork outperform detailed illustrations every time.
pages.heroTitle: Etsy shop icon design rules for logos that survive small screens
pages.heroIntro: Shop icons are tiny trust markers. Clear shapes, high contrast, and centered artwork outperform detailed illustrations every time.
pages.metaTitle: Etsy shop icon design rules for logos that survive small screens | Etsy Image Helper
pages.metaDescription: Shop icons are tiny trust markers. Clear shapes, high contrast, and centered artwork outperform detailed illustrations every time.

sections.heading: Key takeaway
paragraphs: Strip the icon down to one recognizable mark and let negative space do the work.

sections.heading: Practical detail
paragraphs: Small avatars fail when the design relies on fine text or thin decorative elements that disappear on mobile.

sections.heading: Next action
paragraphs: Use the icon preset and confirm your export size before you rebuild the brand mark.

pages.title: Etsy profile photo sizing workflow for founder shots and studio portraits
pages.description: Profile photos should feel warm, legible, and tightly composed. Start with a square crop and make sure the face or subject stays centered.
pages.heroTitle: Etsy profile photo sizing workflow for founder shots and studio portraits
pages.heroIntro: Profile photos should feel warm, legible, and tightly composed. Start with a square crop and make sure the face or subject stays centered.
pages.metaTitle: Etsy profile photo sizing workflow for founder shots and studio portraits | Etsy Image Helper
pages.metaDescription: Profile photos should feel warm, legible, and tightly composed. Start with a square crop and make sure the face or subject stays centered.

sections.heading: Key takeaway
paragraphs: Keep eyes and facial features away from the edges so compression does not cut them off.

sections.heading: Practical detail
paragraphs: Even a simple square portrait looks stronger when the subject is framed with a small margin instead of filling the whole image.

sections.heading: Next action
paragraphs: Check the profile preset in the tool and match the recommended square size before exporting.

pages.title: Etsy order receipt banner setup for clean post-purchase branding
pages.description: The order receipt banner is narrow, so it works best with restrained branding and a single message.
pages.heroTitle: Etsy order receipt banner setup for clean post-purchase branding
pages.heroIntro: The order receipt banner is narrow, so it works best with restrained branding and a single message.
pages.metaTitle: Etsy order receipt banner setup for clean post-purchase branding | Etsy Image Helper
pages.metaDescription: The order receipt banner is narrow, so it works best with restrained branding and a single message.

sections.heading: Key takeaway
paragraphs: Use bold text, simple contrast, and one focal element instead of a busy collage.

sections.heading: Practical detail
paragraphs: Narrow receipt banners collapse fast when they try to carry the full shop identity. Think confirmation strip, not homepage hero.

sections.heading: Next action
paragraphs: Use the receipt banner preset and export a lean layout built for the 760 by 100 shape.

pages.title: Etsy big banner vs mini banner for handmade shops
pages.description: Both banner types can work, but they solve different jobs. One is a statement piece and the other is a lightweight identifier.
pages.heroTitle: Etsy big banner vs mini banner for handmade shops
pages.heroIntro: Both banner types can work, but they solve different jobs. One is a statement piece and the other is a lightweight identifier.
pages.metaTitle: Etsy big banner vs mini banner for handmade shops | Etsy Image Helper
pages.metaDescription: Both banner types can work, but they solve different jobs. One is a statement piece and the other is a lightweight identifier.

sections.heading: When big banner wins
paragraphs: Choose the big banner when the header needs to feel cinematic or when you want to show product context, a tagline, or seasonal art.

sections.heading: When mini banner wins
paragraphs: Choose the mini banner when the shop header just needs a fast brand strip that stays out of the way of listings and categories.

sections.heading: Decision shortcut
paragraphs: If the banner must communicate more than a logo, pick the big banner. If it only needs to reinforce branding, pick the mini banner.

pages.title: Etsy shop icon vs profile photo and when each matters
pages.description: The icon sells the brand mark while the profile photo sells the person behind the shop. Treat them as separate trust layers, not duplicates.
pages.heroTitle: Etsy shop icon vs profile photo and when each matters
pages.heroIntro: The icon sells the brand mark while the profile photo sells the person behind the shop. Treat them as separate trust layers, not duplicates.
pages.metaTitle: Etsy shop icon vs profile photo and when each matters | Etsy Image Helper
pages.metaDescription: The icon sells the brand mark while the profile photo sells the person behind the shop. Treat them as separate trust layers, not duplicates.

sections.heading: When shop icon wins
paragraphs: Use the icon for logos, monograms, or simple illustrations that need to look consistent across every Etsy surface.

sections.heading: When profile photo wins
paragraphs: Use the profile photo when your shop story matters and you want buyers to connect with the maker behind the products.

sections.heading: Decision shortcut
paragraphs: If the surface needs brand recognition, prioritize the icon. If it needs warmth or founder credibility, prioritize the profile photo.

pages.title: Etsy square photos vs vertical photos for catalog consistency
pages.description: Square photos are easier to manage at scale, while vertical photos can feel more editorial when the product demands height.
pages.heroTitle: Etsy square photos vs vertical photos for catalog consistency
pages.heroIntro: Square photos are easier to manage at scale, while vertical photos can feel more editorial when the product demands height.
pages.metaTitle: Etsy square photos vs vertical photos for catalog consistency | Etsy Image Helper
pages.metaDescription: Square photos are easier to manage at scale, while vertical photos can feel more editorial when the product demands height.

sections.heading: When square photos wins
paragraphs: Use square exports when you want consistent thumbnails, faster batch editing, and less unpredictable cropping in category views.

sections.heading: When vertical photos wins
paragraphs: Use vertical compositions when the product needs height, but protect the center area so the image still survives square previews.

sections.heading: Decision shortcut
paragraphs: Default to square for repeatable storefront systems and switch to vertical only when the product story clearly improves.

sections.heading: What is inside

sections.heading: How to use it

sections.heading: Best follow-up

sections.heading: Download it now

sections.heading: Best prompts to collect later
paragraphs: This route stays static-first in the initial release, but it still defines the exact prompts worth collecting once the winning asset earns a lightweight form integration.

The real job of this page is operator clarity: if the site starts attracting repeat tool use or resource downloads, these prompts tell the next build exactly what friction is still slowing the user down.
bullets: Which Etsy surface still causes the most resize churn?
